name: fireworks-ai
description: Run chat completions, text completions, and embeddings on open models via the Fireworks AI inference API

envVars.name: FIREWORKS_API_KEY
envVars.description: Fireworks AI API key from account settings

tools.name: List Models
tools.description: List the models available on Fireworks AI

historicalSummary.omitted: model capability details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Create Chat Completion
tools.description: Generate a chat completion from a list of messages

messages.description: Chat messages, each with role (system|user|assistant) and content

tools.name: Create Completion
tools.description: Generate a text completion from a raw prompt

tools.name: Create Embedding
tools.description: Create an embedding vector for input text

input.description: Text to embed (or an array of strings)

dimensions.description: Number of output dimensions (supported models only)

prompts.title: List Fireworks models
prompts.prompt: List the models available on Fireworks AI for my account.

prompts.title: Generate a response
prompts.prompt: Use Fireworks AI to generate a chat response to a prompt I provide.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Fireworks AI API Setup

steps.title: Create a Fireworks AI account
steps.description: Go to https://app.fireworks.ai and sign up or sign in. New accounts include free credits for testing serverless models.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open the API Keys page under account settings (https://app.fireworks.ai/settings/users/api-keys) and create a new key.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Copy the key and add it to your .env file as FIREWORKS_API_KEY=...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Models tool to confirm the key works. A 401 means the key is wrong or revoked.

notes: The inference API is OpenAI-compatible under https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1
Serverless model IDs use the accounts/fireworks/models/<name> format
Inference is billed per token once free credits are exhausted
